observation: cairn change new scaffolds proposal/design/tasks plus a specs/ dir, but the cairn-propose skill never mentions specs/ or what belongs in it; the scaffold and the skill disagree about the artefact set.
improvement: Either document specs/ in the cairn-propose skill (what goes there, when it is required by cairn accept) or stop scaffolding an empty dir.

observation: cairn-dev documents a `cairn feedback` command logging to .cairn/feedback.md, while this project workflow wants structured JSONL; the built-in feedback channel is prose-only and not machine-aggregatable.
improvement: cairn feedback should accept structured fields (--area, --severity, --json output to a jsonl log) so friction reports can be aggregated across projects.

observation: Blueprint path declarations claim directories, but root-level tooling files (package.json, tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts, demo/) belong to no module; unclear whether cairn scan will flag them as orphans or ignore them, and no documented convention for claiming project-root scaffolding.
improvement: Document a convention (system-level path claims or an explicit ignore list in cairn.config.yaml) for root tooling files.

observation: cairn get/rationale/neighbourhood require fully-qualified node IDs (e.g. hologlyph.runtime.core); the implement-v1 batch contract and task brief refer to modules by short names (core, renderer), causing CAIRN_QUERY_NODE_NOT_FOUND until the namespace is inferred from the suggestions list.
improvement: Accept short module names as aliases in cairn get/rationale/neighbourhood, or document the ID namespace convention in the batch contract so agents know to prefix hologlyph.runtime./hologlyph.asset..

observation: cairn.blueprint core node has outgoing edges only to renderer/behavior/motion/speech/textskin/shaders; it omits audio and asset.loader, yet the implement-v1 batch contract requires core to import and wire createAudioEngine (hologlyph.runtime.audio) and createAssetLoader (hologlyph.asset.loader). The import rule ('only modules with an outgoing edge in cairn.blueprint') therefore forbids imports the factory contract mandates.
improvement: Add outgoing edges core->audio and core->asset.loader to cairn.blueprint so the blueprint matches the batch contract and the import rule is satisfiable without a local workaround.

observation: cairn neighbourhood <node> reports 'Inbound: None / Outbound: None' for module nodes even though cairn.blueprint declares edges touching them (e.g. core->textskin, renderer->textskin, frameworks->web-component, renderer/motion/speech->asset.loader). Independently rediscovered by four of six parallel implementation agents; each was forced to re-read the raw blueprint to verify import-rule compliance.
improvement: Have cairn neighbourhood derive and display inbound/outbound adjacency from the blueprint edge list; it is the single most-needed piece of context for an agent implementing one module against an import rule.

observation: cairn get/neighbourhood show 'Contracts: None' for every node although the whole project codes against a shared contract surface (src/contracts.ts). There is no way to register a contract file so it surfaces in node queries.
improvement: Support a contract declaration at System/Container level (or recognise a conventional shared contract path) and surface it in get/neighbourhood output.

observation: cairn get <node> shows only ID/Name/Description/State; the accepted decisions governing the node require separate decisions/rationale calls. Node IDs must also be fully qualified: short names (core, renderer) fail with CAIRN_QUERY_NODE_NOT_FOUND.
improvement: Include accepted-decision pointers in cairn get, and accept unambiguous short-name aliases for node IDs.

observation: The blueprint edge list was missing core->audio and core->asset.loader even though the engine must instantiate both; the parallel batch contract mandated imports the blueprint forbade. Nothing in cairn flagged the inconsistency; a subagent had to report it as a contract gap.
improvement: cairn could lint 'module imports X but no edge exists' once a JS/TS reconciler lands; until then the cairn-dev skill should tell blueprint authors to check composition-root nodes for completeness of outgoing edges.

observation: cairn scan/hook produced zero orphan findings for a repo that gained ~40 unclaimed files (src/contracts.ts, src/index.ts, demo/, test/, root configs) because cairn files reports language:rust / reconciler_id:rust-code with files:[] for every node in this bun/TypeScript project. Orphan detection is silently inert outside Rust.
improvement: Ship a TS/JS reconciler or emit a loud warning when the reconciler matches zero files for a node whose path exists; a silent pass is worse than an honest 'unsupported language'.

observation: Adding two new edges and two path declarations to cairn.blueprint did NOT trigger CAIRN_BLUEPRINT_CHANGE_NO_DECISION, while the bootstrap session's blueprint replacement did. The rule for when a blueprint change demands a decision artefact is opaque to agents.
improvement: Document the exact trigger conditions for CAIRN_BLUEPRINT_CHANGE_NO_DECISION (node add/remove vs edge/path modification) in finding-codes.md.

observation: Neither cairn-propose nor cairn-apply covers multi-agent parallel implementation. Real coordination failures happened that graph tooling could have prevented: one agent scaffolded placeholder index.ts barrels for sibling modules and clobbered three finished implementations, because nothing owns the file-to-node claim at write time.
improvement: cairn-apply should describe a parallel mode: one agent per frontier node, path claims treated as write ownership, and a pre-merge check that a node's files were only modified by its assigned change/agent.

observation: cairn offers no way to record verification evidence against a change: 103 vitest tests, tsc clean, a live browser smoke test and two adversarial reviews were run for implement-v1, but the change dir only holds prose tasks.md checkboxes cairn cannot read.
improvement: Let cairn accept (or a cairn evidence subcommand) attach gate results (command, exit code, timestamp) to the change so acceptance is grounded in recorded proof.

observation: The --json envelope contract is violated by 13 of 14 commands. SKILL.md states 'All commands with --json produce a consistent envelope: {command, status, data}', but only `cairn check --json` complies. Example: `cairn context --json` begins with an `artefact_counts` object and has no command/status/data keys.
improvement: Wrap every --json response in {command, status, data}, or correct the skill to name exactly which commands use the envelope. Without a status field, JSON consumers cannot distinguish ok/error/partial.

observation: `cairn frontier` lists only 8 of 12 leaf modules, silently dropping hologlyph.runtime.textskin, .shaders, .motion, and .behavior from both Ready and Blocked sets.
improvement: Fix the frontier traversal to include all modules. An agent planning parallel implementation waves via `cairn frontier` would never assign the dropped modules.

observation: `cairn files <node> --json` reports language: rust, reconciler_id: rust-code for every module in a bun/TypeScript/three.js repository; all targets return files: [] with an identical hash regardless of path.
improvement: Add a TypeScript/JS reconciler (detect package.json/tsconfig.json) or document the default reconciler and per-node language override.

observation: No ghost/empty-module signal exists: cairn context showed 16 healthy nodes and 0 warnings while every module path was an empty directory. cairn frontier tracks ghost state but nothing else surfaces it.
improvement: Add a CAIRN_GHOST_MODULE finding or has_code field in files/frontier output so implemented and ghost nodes are distinguishable.

observation: No command lists all project todos: `cairn todos <system-root>` returns None, bare `cairn todos` errors with CAIRN_CLI_MISSING_NODE, and neighbourhood --include-todos is neighbourhood-scoped (showed 5 of 6 todos for core).
improvement: Make `cairn todos <system>` aggregate descendants or add `cairn todos --all`.

observation: `cairn status` and `cairn neighbourhood --include-changes` print 'Active changes: None' while `cairn changes` lists implement-v1. Conflicting signals about whether a change is active.
improvement: Reconcile the definition of 'active change' across changes, status, and neighbourhood.

observation: Change progress in meta/changes/implement-v1/tasks.md checkboxes is invisible to cairn: `cairn show implement-v1` prints 'Summary: no operations' and --json exposes no progress or gate fields.
improvement: Parse tasks.md checkboxes into change progress or document that todo artefacts are the source of truth; add a read-only gate-readiness view.

observation: No read-only preview of acceptance-gate status exists: accept has no --dry-run and `cairn show --json` has no gate fields, so the only way to learn gate outcome is the mutating `cairn accept`.
improvement: Add gate status to `cairn show` or an `accept --dry-run` flag.

observation: `cairn rationale <node>` over-includes: for hologlyph.runtime.speech it returned all 8 project decisions including ones where speech is neither member nor neighbour (dec.text-skin, dec.renderer-posture, dec.scroll-timeline), while `cairn decisions` scopes correctly. Inclusion rule undocumented.
improvement: Constrain rationale to member decisions or clearly flag transitive ones so agents don't infer a decision governs a module it doesn't.

observation: `cairn order` places parent containers after their child modules (asset.loader at 5, hologlyph.asset at 14) while other containers precede their children; containment ordering is inconsistent.
improvement: Stabilise container-before-child ordering or document that order reflects only dependency edges.

observation: cairn-apply's verification gates are Rust-only (cargo build/clippy/fmt/test); none apply to this bun/TypeScript project, compounding the rust reconciler misidentification.
improvement: Make cairn-apply language-aware (derive gates from package.json scripts) or state it assumes Rust.

observation: cairn-apply references acceptance gate 'CC002 for suggested edges' but no CC code is defined anywhere (references/finding-codes.md only lists 'CC - CLI findings' as a category).
improvement: Define CC002 and the other CC codes in finding-codes.md, including how to satisfy the suggested-edges gate.

observation: The cairn-dev command table omits ~17 commands present in `cairn --help`: frontier, next, brief, bundle, health, workspace, backlog, drafts and draft_* verbs, gap, docstring, symbols, summarise, remediate, rename, import-openspec, watch. Several (frontier, next, health) are exactly what an agent needs for wave planning.
improvement: Document these commands, prioritising frontier/next for planning and health for pre-commit verification.

observation: references/finding-codes.md is incomplete: CAIRN_SOURCE_UNVERIFIED (emitted by cairn check in this very repo) is not defined there.
improvement: Document every emittable finding code.

observation: Exit-code documentation mismatch: SKILL.md says '1 = success with findings or operational error' while CLI help says '1 = blocking findings, or command failed'; non-blocking info findings exit 0 in practice.
improvement: Align the skill text with actual semantics (0 = no blocking findings).

observation: `cairn todo new` stamped created: 2026-07-09 on 2026-07-10; wrong day in frontmatter.
improvement: Use the actual current date when stamping created.

observation: `cairn change new` scaffolds a specs/ directory that neither the cairn-propose skill nor any cairn command references; it stayed empty through the whole change lifecycle.
improvement: Either give specs/ a documented role in the accept gate or stop scaffolding it.

observation: The TypeScript reconciler only activates when a blueprint node claims a .ts FILE directly (path './src/contracts.ts'); with directory-only path claims, cairn files reports language:rust/files:[] and orphan detection is silent. Adding one file claim suddenly surfaced ~15 CAIRN_RECONCILE_ORPHANED_FILE findings repo-wide, and removing it made them vanish again.
improvement: Activate the TS reconciler from directory claims too (detect tsconfig.json/package.json), and make reconciler selection deterministic per project rather than per claimed-path shape.

observation: CT001 'Interface contradiction: targets with contract role public_api have divergent interfaces' fires for ANY node with two path targets whose exports differ, making it impossible to claim a module dir plus its test file, a shared contracts.ts, or a root barrel (src/index.ts) on the same node. There is no documented way to claim a target with a non-public_api role.
improvement: Allow per-path role annotations (e.g. path './test/core.test.ts' role 'tests') or exempt declared test/tooling globs from public_api interface unification; document CT001 (it is absent from finding-codes.md).

observation: cairn hook all exited 0 while cairn scan reported 10 Error-severity CT001 findings in one configuration, then exited 1 for 2 of the same findings after a blueprint edit; the blocking relationship between scan errors and hook decisions was not consistent or explainable from the docs.
improvement: Make hook blocking a pure function of scan error findings, or print which findings the hook decision counted.

observation: cairn accept implement-v1 --json returned gate_outcome:failed because the acceptance gate hardcodes cargo build/clippy/fmt/test; there is no Cargo.toml here and no way to configure project gates (cairn.config.yaml has no gates section, accept has no flags). The change therefore can never pass acceptance in a TS project despite tsc/vitest/build/hook all green.
improvement: Read gate commands from cairn.config.yaml (or package.json scripts) per project; fall back to language detection. Until then cairn-apply should warn that accept is Rust-only.

observation: cairn init scaffolds `reconcilers: rust-code` unconditionally in cairn.config.yaml regardless of project language; this is why reconciliation and orphan detection misbehaved all session in a TypeScript repo.
improvement: cairn init should detect the project language (package.json/tsconfig vs Cargo.toml) and scaffold the matching reconciler, or scaffold none and say so.

observation: cairn accept --help (and other per-command --help) falls back to the global command list; there is no per-command usage/flag documentation at all.
improvement: Implement per-command --help output.

observation: Positive: `cairn feedback` worked well as a lightweight friction channel (recorded to .cairn/feedback.md and generated a prefilled upstream issue URL), but it truncated the issue title mid-word and offers no structured fields, so this project needed a parallel meta/cairn-feedback.jsonl for machine-readable logging.
improvement: Add --area/--severity flags and a --json output mode to cairn feedback; do not truncate the generated issue title mid-word.

observation: The scaffolded .cairn/AGENTS.md is not regenerated when cairn is upgraded (0.1.4 -> 0.2.0), so it still documents pre-0.2.0 verbs like `cairn changes` that the installed CLI rejects (`unknown command 'changes'`). Any agent following the scaffolded guide after an upgrade runs broken commands.
improvement: Regenerate or version-stamp .cairn/AGENTS.md on `cairn` upgrade (or at least on first post-upgrade command), and print a staleness warning when the scaffolded guide's generated-by version differs from the installed CLI.

observation: `cairn context` reports 'Backlog: 0 ready' while 8 open todos with satisfies: v2-embodiment exist and `cairn todos <node>` lists them; the backlog counter appears to count only beads, not open todo artefacts, so the headline readiness signal contradicts the todo tree.
improvement: Make the context Backlog line aggregate open todos (or label it 'Beads:' if it is beads-only) so agents do not conclude there is no planned work.

observation: After upgrading 0.1.4 -> 0.2.0, the init-scaffolded cairn.config.yaml `reconcilers:` key raises CAIRN_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEY on every scan. The scaffold wrote a rust-code reconciler into a TypeScript repo (already filed) and the upgrade offers no config migration.
improvement: Migrate or at least rewrite known-obsolete config keys on first post-upgrade run, or ship a `cairn config migrate` command.

observation: `cairn brief` (0.2.0) correctly fuses the next open todo with binding decisions, but its 'Gates that will judge this unit' block is cargo-hardcoded (cargo build/clippy/test) in a TypeScript repo, same family as the accept-gate hardcoding in #234. It also appends a beads/Dolt note (`bd ready` is authoritative) irrelevant to repos not using beads.
improvement: Derive the brief's gate list from repo language/config (or a declared gates block), and suppress the beads note when no beads backend is configured.

observation: `cairn brief <node-id>` errors with 'No bead matches hologlyph.asset.pipeline' - the positional argument is interpreted as a bead id, not a node, while bare `cairn brief` selects from native todos. Inconsistent with `cairn todos <node>` / `cairn get <node>` argument conventions.
improvement: Accept a node id (or --node flag) in `cairn brief` and scope todo selection to it, matching the rest of the CLI surface.

observation: cairn change accept omp-project-config runs a cargo-hardcoded verification battery (cargo build/clippy/fmt/test) in this TypeScript repo and fails with could-not-find-Cargo.toml; also cairn lint --strict omp-project-config fails after the change was archived
improvement: select the verification battery from the reconciler/language of the repo (typescript-code -> tsc/vitest) instead of hardcoding cargo; make accept resolvable for archived changes or say so explicitly
